steps.title: Use the given angle pair
steps.content: The triangles already have one matching angle pair: angle A and angle P each measure $54^\circ$. To prove similarity, it is enough to establish one more matching angle pair.

steps.title: Test the angle information
steps.content:
If angle B is $38^\circ$, then the third angle in triangle ABC is

$$180^\circ-54^\circ-38^\circ=88^\circ$$

If angle R is $88^\circ$, then the third angle in triangle PQR is

$$180^\circ-54^\circ-88^\circ=38^\circ$$

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Both triangles then have angle measures $54^\circ$, $38^\circ$, and $88^\circ$, so they are similar by angle-angle similarity. The answer is C.
